perturbation.original_text: Maintenance Services No-charge Maintenance Services shall be provided from the Delivery Date through the Warranty Period. The first paid (Initial) Maintenance Term shall commence upon expiration of the Warranty Period and shall continue for twelve (12) months thereafter. Thereafter, the Maintenance Term shall automatically renew for successive period, 12 months, on the terms and conditions of this Agreement unless Bank of America terminates Maintenance Services pursuant to this Agreement. Bank of America may terminate Maintenance Services for convenience at any time in accordance with the Section entitled "Termination" of the Agreement. If Bank of America terminates the Maintenance Services, Bank of America shall have the right to reinstate the Maintenance Services without paying any reinstatement fee. During the initial Maintenance Term and any renewal term, Maintenance Fees shall be paid in the increments described below under "Payment Terms."
perturbation.changed_text: Maintenance Services No-charge Maintenance Services shall be provided from the Delivery Date through the Warranty Period. The first paid (Initial) Maintenance Term shall commence upon expiration of the Warranty Period and shall continue for twelve (12) months thereafter. Thereafter, the Maintenance Term shall automatically renew as Bank of America sees fit, on terms and conditions that will be decided at the moment of renewal, unless Bank of America terminates Maintenance Services pursuant to this Agreement. Bank of America may terminate Maintenance Services for convenience at any time in accordance with the Section entitled "Termination" of the Agreement. If Bank of America terminates the Maintenance Services, Bank of America shall have the right to reinstate the Maintenance Services without paying any reinstatement fee. During the initial Maintenance Term and any renewal term, Maintenance Fees shall be paid in the increments described below under "Payment Terms."
perturbation.explanation: The original text specified that the maintenance term renews for successive 12-month periods. The modified text replaces this with a renewal process dictated by Bank of America under new terms at the time of renewal. This introduces an in-text contradiction because the later reference to 'Maintenance Fees shall be paid in the increments described below under "Payment Terms"' becomes ambiguous. If the renewal terms are decided at the moment of renewal, the 'Payment Terms' may no longer be applicable or relevant, and creates an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Maintenance Services No-charge Maintenance Services shall be provided from the Delivery Date through the Warranty Period. The first paid (Initial) Maintenance Term shall commence upon expiration of the Warranty Period and shall continue for twelve (12) months thereafter. Thereafter, the Maintenance Term shall automatically renew as Bank of America sees fit, on terms and conditions that will be decided at the moment of renewal, unless Bank of America terminates Maintenance Services pursuant to this Agreement. Bank of America may terminate Maintenance Services for convenience at any time in accordance with the Section entitled "Termination" of the Agreement. If Bank of America terminates the Maintenance Services, Bank of America shall have the right to reinstate the Maintenance Services without paying any reinstatement fee. During the initial Maintenance Term and any renewal term, Maintenance Fees shall be paid in the increments described below under "Payment Terms."
perturbation.justification: The modified text alters the renewal terms of the Maintenance Term, stating it will renew 'as Bank of America sees fit, on terms and conditions that will be decided at the moment of renewal.' This directly contradicts the original implication in the contradicted text that 'Maintenance Fees shall be paid in the increments described below under "Payment Terms."', creating an in-text contradiction because the payment terms might no longer be applicable or relevant under the new renewal terms.

perturbation.original_text: WARRANTY PERIOD DURATION Delivery Date: TBD Installation Date: TBD The period commencing on the Installation Date and continuing for the number of days specified: 120 days Notwithstanding anything set forth elsewhere in this Agreement (or below), the Maintenance Period shall be twenty- four (24) hours per day, seven (7) days per week, including Bank of America holidays. 120 days
perturbation.changed_text: WARRANTY PERIOD DURATION Delivery Date: TBD Installation Date: TBD The period commencing on the Installation Date and continuing for the number of days specified: 120 days Notwithstanding anything set forth elsewhere in this Agreement (or below), the Maintenance Period shall be determined upon agreement on both parties. 120 days
perturbation.explanation: The original agreement states that maintenance period is 24/7. However, the change now puts the condition that 'Maintenance Period shall be determined upon agreement on both parties', which causes uncertainty in enforcement. The '120 days' remains unchanged after the removal of original agreement for maintenance period, creating an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: WARRANTY PERIOD DURATION Delivery Date: TBD Installation Date: TBD The period commencing on the Installation Date and continuing for the number of days specified: 120 days Notwithstanding anything set forth elsewhere in this Agreement (or below), the Maintenance Period shall be determined upon agreement on both parties. 120 days
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies that the Maintenance Period is 24/7. The changed text introduces uncertainty by stating that the Maintenance Period will be 'determined upon agreement on both parties.' This contradicts the fixed 24/7 maintenance period that was previously defined, creating a strong in-text contradiction as it allows for a maintenance period that could be less than 24/7, making the original definition unenforceable. This aligns with the definition of 'Omissions – In Text Contradiction' because essential language defining the maintenance period has been altered, creating a contradiction. The contradicted text includes '120 days', which is still present in the changed text.
